task: Verify the core blind-debate experience through the real browser UI.
notes: This file is the browser-enforced subset of the broader LSD v1.2 design criteria.
It focuses on requirements that are already represented in the current app surface and can be verified end to end through the UI.

criteria.title: Create a debate from the UI from a blank-start snapshot state
checks: When the app starts without an active debate, the create-debate form is visible.
Submitting a resolution and scope creates the active debate and unlocks posting.
The public posting surface remains identity-blind and centered on arguments rather than contributors.

criteria.title: Submit opposing argument units from the UI
checks: A FOR post can be submitted from the browser.
An AGAINST post can be submitted from the browser.
The pending-post count and list reflect both submissions.

criteria.title: Generate an immutable scoring snapshot from the UI
checks: Generating a snapshot from the browser succeeds.
Pending posts reset after the snapshot completes.
Snapshot metadata and verdict data appear in the page header.

criteria.title: Inspect topic and verdict decision surfaces through the UI
checks: The Topics page renders at least one topic row after a snapshot.
The Verdict page renders overall scores and topic contributions after a snapshot.
